top_signals.title: UAE's exit from OPEC+ reduced the group's share of crude oil production and capacity
top_signals.summary: On April 28, 2026, the United Arab Emirates (UAE) announced that it was leaving OPEC, effective on May 1. OPEC was formed in 1960 by Iraq, Iran, Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, and Venezuela, with the stated objective to "coordinate and unify petroleum policies among Member Countries." OPEC is best known for its effect on global crude oil prices.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: NATO to add up to five Northrop Grumman Triton drones for maritime surveillance
top_signals.summary: “It is genuinely made in NATO and is creating jobs on both sides of the Atlantic,” alliance Secretary-General Mark Rutte said.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Has Iraq Finally Run Out of Road in Its Double-Dealings with the West?
top_signals.summary: Late June/early July normally brings twin events from Iraq for the global oil industry: an announcement that it intends to increase its crude oil production to either 6 or 7 million barrels per day (bpd) within three years, and a statement that its prime minister will visit Washington to discuss deepening strategic ties or something similar aimed at securing money from the U.S. This year is no different, with the announced three-year target being 7 million bpd, and the visit of new prime minister, Ali al-Zaidi happening in the middle of this month.…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Meta releases first image model since Zuckerberg’s AI overhaul
top_signals.summary: Muse Spark Image will be integrated into tech giant’s AI chatbot and its Instagram photo app
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Britain can’t afford a self-harming tourist tax
top_signals.summary: Tourism is exactly the kind of industry that the country needs more of, yet it’s about to be taxed like a burden instead, says Anne Strickland With millions of us packing for summer holidays this month, it is worth remembering that tourism in the United Kingdom is one of the industries that we are still [...]

top_signals.title: 1kx, Blockchain Capital back $7.5 million KOR Protocol Series A at $100 million valuation
top_signals.summary: KOR Protocol is building an onchain clearinghouse that helps register and route creative works and pay out rights holders.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
